title: Sith apparel
content:
{{Top|mprot|leg|canon=Sith robe}} {{Conjecture|legends}} {{Cleanup}} [[File:Sith_apparel.png|thumb|right|200px|The Dark Lords Darth Vader, Darth Maul, and Darth Sidious]] {{Quote|You wear the trappings of the Sith, you fight like the Sith… but this can be imitated, however.|Dooku, to Asajj Ventress|{{CW|7}}}} A type of '''apparel''' was the general [[Clothing/Legends|attire]] of members of the [[Sith Order (disambiguation)|Sith Order]] in all its forms, with most [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] wearing '''Sith robes'''. Unlike the Jedi who did not believe in material possession, the position of [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] was highly individualized and the personal attire of each Dark Lord reflected this. Although similar to the [[Jedi apparel/Legends|clothing and armor]] worn by the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]], Sith apparel was in fact much more personalized to the wearer than its [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] counterparts; for many [[Sith/Legends|Sith]], their armor and robes were an integral part of their identity. For users of the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]], their clothing and armor usually involved some measure of physical protection, such as traditional armor plates, [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]], or even self-contained life support systems. Many Sith were most easily recognized by their distinctive garb and often used this to further intimidate or impress their foes and allies alike. ==History== ===The Old Sith Empire=== {{Quote|Marka Ragnos, great Dark Lord, wear this victory helmet during your battles in the afterlife.|Ludo Kressh|[[Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Golden Age of the Sith'' 2]]}} [[File:NagaSadow-NEGTC.png|thumb|left|160px|The personalized Sith armor worn by Naga Sadow]] The first [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lords]] were [[Exiles|fallen]] [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] who enslaved the [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith]], a [[Species/Legends|species]] hailing from [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]], a planet in the [[Stygian Caldera]].''[[Book of Sith: Secrets from the Dark Side]]'' This resulted in a mixed-race population with mixed cultural traits. The apparel of the [[Sith Order (disambiguation)|Sith Order]] combined inspirations from both the Jedi,[[Legacy (2006) 30|''Legacy'' (2006) 30]] the guardians of peace and justice in the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]],{{Film|IV}} and the Sith. Unlike their Jedi forefathers, the Sith Lords enjoyed flaunting riches and outstanding signs of their wealth.''[[Darth Bane: Path of Destruction]]'' They adopted a fastuous fashion full of bright [[Color/Legends|colors]] such as green, light red or purple, with ornamented parts of [[Gold/Legends|golden]] [[Armor/Legends|armor]]. They also favored extravagant headdresses, war helmets and capes accented with eccentric necklines. [[Naga Sadow/Legends|Naga Sadow]] and [[Ludo Kressh]] were good examples of this. Most Sith Lords would feel pleasure and a sensation of power through the jealousy and greed their material possession aroused among their peers. This attitude was still present amongst the Sith millennia later—Master [[Qordis]] was known to wear only the finest silks and most expensive jewelry, and he possessed luxurious apartments on [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]]. Robes worn by Sith were known as Sith robes. Many Sith robes were [[The Force/Legends|Force]]-imbued in the making, so that they could be used to enhance or assist one's ability to channel the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side of the Force]] and call upon more strength or wisdom than they naturally would. Some of those artifacts were still intact and usable at the time of the [[Old Sith Wars]], such as [[Aleema Keto's robe|Aleema Keto's]] or [[Crado's robe|Crado's]] robes,''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic II: The Sith Lords]]'' and the dreaded [[Helm of Dathka Graush]].[[Knights of the Old Republic 29|''Knights of the Old Republic'' 29]] Around [[5000 BBY/Legends|5000 BBY]],''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' some of the [[Massassi/Legends|Massassi]] [[Sith Warrior|warriors]] who accompanied Naga Sadow on [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]][[Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire 5|''Tales of the Jedi – The Fall of the Sith Empire'' 5]] were also known to sport [[Jungle combat gear|specialized combat gear]] that allowed them maximum agility in dense jungle environments. At an unknown point in history, [[Sith Warrior|Sith warriors]] wore robes designed with a hood which could conceal the wearer's face, keeping them from being identified. Some Sith Robes also came fitted with basic armor made of cortosis. These robes shared some similar fashion features with the [[Jedi apparel/Legends|Jedi Adventure Robe]] and the [[Jedi apparel/Legends|Ceremonial Jedi Robes]].''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]'' ===Battle armor=== {{Quote|Yes, you shall wear the armor.|Unidentified Sith spirit, to Warb Null|''[[Tales of the Jedi Companion]]''}} [[File:Warb_Null_armor.png|thumb|200px|Warb Null built his own set of Sith armor.]] The Sith Lords of the [[Sith Empire/Legends|Old Empire]] each wore personalized battle [[Armor/Legends|armor]] designed to strike fear into their enemies.{{DB|technology|darthvaderssuit|Darth Vader's suit}} Each [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] tended to have their unique set of armor, though [[Ancient Sith armor|standard models]] were known to exist.''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' The Sith armors traditionally consisted of metal plates worn over dark robes, and were usually covered in sharp spines to be used as weapons. Some time prior to [[3998 BBY]], the young [[Onderon/Legends|Onderonian]] [[Metallurgy/Legends|metallurgist]] [[Warb Null|Shas Dovos]] made his own suit of Sith armor out of steel plates, following the instruction of an ancient book. Dovos later merged with his armor, turning him into a new and powerful being known as Warb Null. Millennia later, [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] [[Darth Vader's armor/Legends|heavy suit]] would follow this tradition of donning intimidating and fearsome-looking protective coverings. If the latter followed closely the making process of the ancient armors, it is quite possible they were made through the use of [[Sith alchemy/Legends|Sith Alchemy]], in order to augment the user's physical strength and vitality.''[[The Dark Side Sourcebook]]'' ===The Brotherhood of the Sith=== After the Dark Lord [[Exar Kun/Legends|Exar Kun]][[Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith 6|''Tales of the Jedi – Dark Lords of the Sith'' 6]] and his [[Shadow Hand]] [[Ulic Qel-Droma/Legends|Ulic Qel-Droma]]{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/member/insideronline/88/indexp2.html|text=''Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties''|int=Evil Never Dies: The Sith Dynasties}} established their [[Brotherhood of the Sith]],[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 2|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 2]] the first order to their new students was the [[Sith uprising]], in which the apprentices were commanded to strike down their Jedi masters.[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 3|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 3]] As such, very few of them had the chance to change their Jedi garments which led to a yet less uniformed Sith Order.[[Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War 4|''Tales of the Jedi – The Sith War'' 4]] Kun himself wore a [[Exar Kun's light battle suit|light battle suit]].''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' ===Revan's Sith Empire=== [[File:Darth_Malak.png|thumb|left|160px|Darth Malak wore a skintight crimson bodysuit that showed off his powerful physique.]] At the time where Revan founded his new empire, the Sith likely chose to get more practical robes. The technology itself had known fundamental improvements in the previous decades, and gave a more austere and high-tech look to the whole Galaxy. This is probably why individuals such as [[Darth Bandon]], [[Darth Malak]] or even Revan himself wore such unadorned sets of clothes. [[Revan/Legends|Darth Revan]] himself owned a special set of armored robes, including a [[Revan's mask|full helmet]] of Mandalorian design. The many renegade [[Dark Jedi/Legends|Dark Jedi]] who joined Revan simply clung to [[Jedi apparel/Legends|Jedi garment style]], adapted to the dark shades of their new allegiance. [[Darth Revan's Sith Empire]] also had huge numbers of minions at its command, including the [[Sith trooper (Sith Empire)|Sith assault troopers]] and the [[Sith assassin/Legends|Sith Assassins]], whose outfits were highly specialized. In the aftermath of the [[Mandalorian Wars]], the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]] officers who served under [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] turned and joined the new Sith Empire. They replaced their red and yellow uniforms with azure blue/gray jumpsuits, with higher ranking officers wearing caps. This uniform also came to be worn by the trainees at the [[Sith Academy (Korriban)|Sith Academy]]. ===The Great Galactic War=== Sometime after the [[Great Galactic War]], a Sith robe was a popular choice of garment for Sith serving the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted Sith Empire]] and the [[Tenebrae|Sith Emperor]] during the [[Cold War (Galactic Republic–Sith Empire)|Cold War]] between the Sith and the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]]. It was heavier than what Sith usually wore before and after the events of the Great War and the Cold War. The [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] developed a similar [[Jedi apparel/Legends|garment]] which was equipped by their military forces. They also had a special type of gauntlet, called [[warrior's gloves]], that they wore during the Cold War. ===The New Sith Wars=== {{Quote|If this is all we have to stand against Lord Hoth, we may as was well burn our black robes and start practicing the Jedi Code.|[[Kopecz]]|}} Throughout the [[New Sith Wars]], the style of the Sith apparel evolved into a more sober form, though it still had a touch of the old elaborate fashion of the ancient Sith Lords. Brighter colors were left behind in favor of different shades of black, dark purple and gray. The Sith would generally wear black capes with cuirasses and multi-pouched belts with buckles featuring symbols similar to [[Sith tattoo]]s. Lord [[Skere Kaan]] and [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]] themselves were often seen dressed that way, and Bane even wore an armored pauldron and a vambrace to protect his left arm.''[[Star Wars: Jedi vs. Sith]]'' [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]] later wore biological armor made from hard-shelled [[orbalisk]]s which, through a symbiotic relationship, increased his Force abilities.''[[Darth Bane: Rule of Two]]'' ===Order of the Sith Lords=== {{Quote|Why are you hiding inside the cowl of your cloak?''"
"''It is the clothing of my Order, Viceroy.|Nute Gunray and Sidious|[[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']]}} [[File:DarthSidious-IWtbaJ.png|thumb|200px|Darth Sidious wore dark, concealing Sith robes.]] The Sith Lords of the [[Order of the Sith Lords]] were in hiding and generally favored simpler black garments. Their concealing black robes made them appear as if they were creatures of the night.[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'' novelization]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]] favored monk-like robes made of harsh [[Zeyd-cloth/Legends|zeyd-cloth]].''[[Star Wars: Beware the Sith]]'' His prefered robe was black, with a raised waffle pattern on it and long sleeves that reached to just below the wrists.{{Film|III}}{{Film|VI}} This gave the illusion, by the time of [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], that Palpatine, the alter-ego of Sidious, had merely been a simple man.''[[Star Wars: The Visual Dictionary]]''''[[Star Wars: The Complete Visual Dictionary]]'' Along with his black robe, Palpatine possessed several sets of Sith robes. One set was similar to his favored black robes but was made of a burgandy material.''[[Labyrinth of Evil]]'' Another was made in a burgundy velvet-like fabric, ornamented with Sith symbols. Palpatine publicly wore it in front of the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Senate]] when he addressed his [[Declaration of a New Order]]. Indeed, he knew nobody in the [[Senate Building/Legends|rotunda]] would recognize the origin of these clothes, and he estimated the time of hiding had come to an end for the Sith.''[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith: The Visual Dictionary]]'' Another was a gray robe with a large hood, a black velvet capelet and puffed sleeves as seen when he battled [[Yoda/Legends|Yoda]]. When the reborn Sidious went on a trip to [[Korriban/Legends|Korriban]] in [[11 ABY/Legends|11 ABY]], he adorned a shorter, more practical variant of his well-known black cape, complete with capelet and a long, straight walking stick.[[Empire's End 2|''Empire's End'' 2]] [[Sith apprentice/Legends|Sith apprentices]] wore special training gear during their [[Sith training|training]] to become Sith Lords. An example of such training gear was worn by [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]], and consisted of a uniquely tailored vest. Once Maul acquired the rank of [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]], he wore the black garb of the [[Sith Order (disambiguation)|Sith Order]]. In a more elaborate fashion than the standard zeyd-cloth cloaks, [[Darth Maul/Legends|Darth Maul]] wore black robes and loose-fitting desert-like garb, and a collared coat belted at the waist. The very large hood would drape down the back while the sleeves were elongated and almost touched the ground. Where the sleeves attached to the main body of the robe, two slits were cut, through which Maul could put his arms.''[[Star Wars: Episode I: The Visual Dictionary]]'' [[File:DarthVader-SWBC52.png|thumb|left|150px|Though in his original Jedi attire, Darth Vader's robes share a similarity to the hooded cloaks of the Sith.]] [[Dooku/Legends|Darth Tyranus]], Sidious' second apprentice, retained the traditional garb of the [[Count/Legends|Counts]] of [[Serenno/Legends|Serenno]]. It consisted of a dark brown double-breasted [[Vjun tunic]], a pair of horse-riding pants and an [[Armorweave/Legends|armorweave]] cape—the shape of which would enlarge the Count's silhouette. He also wore black [[Boot/Legends|boots]] made of rare [[Rancor/Legends|rancor]] [[Leather/Legends|leather]], reaching just below the knees.''[[Star Wars: Attack of the Clones: The Visual Dictionary]]'' Though [[Asajj Ventress/Legends|Asajj Ventress]] was not a Sith per se, she wore "the trappings of the Sith," as Dooku himself noted. Her gear consisted of high-collared tight-fitting black and white shirt and leggings under a flowing black skirt. The skirt was easily removable by simply tucking the [[Rattataki sash|sash]] away. When [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] needed to be put in the suit that he would later be known for, Darth Sidious insisted on creating an intimidating look and completed the design with a cape and tabard reminiscent of the traditional Sith garb.[[Star Wars: Revenge of the Sith (novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode III ''Revenge of the Sith'' novelization]]''[[Dark Lord: The Rise of Darth Vader]]'' Vader himself provided his [[Galen Marek|Secret Apprentice]] with an Imperial uniform that he mended himself many times over his lifetime. Starkiller's training gear was both simple and versatile seeing as it survived numerous training sessions and volatile missions. Starkiller used variants of his basic gear, such as the homemade Heavy training gear which provided protection in toxic and polluted environments. Another was the Light training gear which was suited for use in humid environments.[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed (novelization)|''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed'' novelization]] ===One Sith=== Sith of [[Darth Krayt]]'s [[One Sith]] wore armor of various designs. Darth Krayt himself wore an [[Vonduun Skerr Kyrric|imposing form of armor]] created by the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] that was designed to instill fear into the hearts of his enemies. [[Darth Wyyrlok (III)|Darth Wyyrlok]] wore a set of combat armor over his robes, while [[Darth Nihl]] wore a strange set of armor designed to intimidate enemies. ==Behind the scenes== '''Sith robes production design''' As George Lucas envisioned the Sith as the dark counterpart of the Jedi, their robes were mainly designed as black versions of the Jedi apparel. The under-robes for the costume of Palpatine as an Emperor were in fact a Japanese kimono of black raw silk, as revealed in [[Star Wars: The Magic of Myth (book)|''Star Wars: The Magic of Myth'']] book. Ben Kenobi's inner robes were also made of the same material, but in a beige version. Darth Maul's attire was sewn from a hand woven fabric called Indian Homespun which had been dyed black by costume designer Trisha Biggar. '''''Tales of the Jedi'' design''' Though the timespan covered by the Tales of the Jedi stories kind of fits "Star Wars' Middle Age", the style of the costumes rather reflects Antiquity. As for the Sith, they seem to be widely inspired by ancient Babylonian or Egyptian fashion; for example, Naga Sadow's and Darth Andeddu's headdress are quite similar to Pharaonic [[Wikipedia:Deshret|deshret]] and [[Wikipedia:Khepresh|khepresh]] crowns. Also, rather than be limited to dark colors, the robes of these ancient Sith were quite vibrantly colored, extremely opulent. ==Appearances== {{ScrollBox| {{IncompleteList}} *[[Tales of the Jedi – The
